name: Chiang Mai Airport frontage and ride-hailing band
notes: Airport-adjacent frontage covering terminal access, ride-hailing pickup, rental-car turnover, Central Airport spillover, and hotel/receiver traffic. AOT passenger throughput and landside circulation justify stronger year-round demand than a generic destination zone.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 2 west-southwest urban fringe
notes: Ring 2 / Chom.3029 stretch covering Mae Hia, early Hang Dong, canal-road frontage, and mixed urban expansion demand suited to destination charging.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 2 north-northeast urban fringe
notes: Ring 2 / Chom.3029 stretch covering Fa Ham, Nong Chom, San Sai edge, and community retail growth along the urban fringe.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 2 east-southeast urban fringe
notes: Ring 2 / Chom.3029 stretch covering inner San Kamphaeng and Saraphi spillover where city activity blends with suburban housing and roadside retail.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 3 south-southeast connector
notes: Highway 121 outer-ring section linking Hang Dong, Saraphi, and south-side cross-town trips without entering the inner city.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 3 east connector
notes: Highway 121 outer-ring east side connecting Doi Saket, San Kamphaeng, Lampang-side flow, and logistics-style corridor demand.

name: Chiang Mai Ring 3 north connector
notes: Highway 121 outer-ring north side linking San Sai, Mae Rim directions, and inter-district traffic that bypasses the inner city.
